name: Gemini 3.1 Pro

price.provider: Google AI Studio / Vertex AI (Gemini API)

note: Coding-arena leader with extended long-context frontier reasoning; ARC-AGI-2 77.1%, 1M-token context.

results.notes: ARC-AGI-2, verified score, more than double Gemini 3 Pro
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (51.4% with search+code execution). Confirmed on both deepmind.google/models/gemini/pro/ and deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-1-pro/.
GDPval-AA, Elo-style rating for expert/professional tasks.
tau2-bench Telecom domain (labeled 'τ2-bench Telecom' on the page). Page also shows a separate 'τ2-bench Retail' score of 90.8%, which has no matching taxonomy slug and was left out.
BrowseComp agentic web-browsing benchmark, Search+Python+Browse tool setting.
Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Gemini CLI harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 15.8% / Score 32.0% (rank 34). Score component used per corpus convention.
MMMLU (Multilingual MMLU), single attempt. Confirmed via direct fetch of the official model page; sits in the same performance table as the 12 benchmarks already tracked but was not yet captured.

τ2-bench Retail domain (labeled 'τ2-bench Retail' on the page, alongside the tracked τ2-bench Telecom score). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]
Score extracted from the page's embedded data-target HTML attribute since a plain static fetch only shows client-side-animated placeholder values.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via blog.google (2026-02-19 launch) and deepmind.google/models/gemini/pro/ (fetched directly). All 5 benchmark numbers (GPQA 94.3, ARC-AGI-2 77.1, SWE-bench 80.6, MMMU-Pro 80.5, LiveCodeBench Pro 2887 Elo) literally confirmed on the cited pages. 1M context and $2/$12 pricing confirmed via independent sources. Still in preview as of July 2026 (not yet GA) per multiple July-2026 press coverage — status left as preview, correctly. No changes to record.

[2026-07-16] Researched the six missing core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu). Checked Google's official Feb-2026 model card (deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-1-pro/) and its companion evals-methodology PDF (storage.googleapis.com/deepmind-media/gemini/gemini_3-1_pro_model_evaluation.pdf, downloaded and text-extracted directly), plus Vals AI's independent per-benchmark leaderboard (vals.ai/models/google_gemini-3.1-pro-preview, fetched as raw HTML to read the embedded data-target score attributes since the static page otherwise renders placeholder 0.0% values). Google's official table covers a fixed set of ~16 benchmarks (HLE, ARC-AGI-2, GPQA Diamond, Terminal-Bench 2.0, SWE-Bench Verified/Pro, LiveCodeBench Pro, SciCode, APEX-Agents, GDPval-AA, tau2-bench Retail/Telecom, MCP Atlas, BrowseComp, MMMU-Pro, MMMLU, MRCR v2 128k/1M) and never mentions MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval by name anywhere in either the model card or the eval methodology doc -- these have been superseded in Google's reporting by MMMLU, LiveCodeBench Pro, and SWE-Bench respectively. Notably even AIME, which sibling model Gemini 3 Pro's model card does report (95.0%), is absent from Gemini 3.1 Pro's table, indicating a deliberate reporting-set change rather than an oversight. Vals AI tracks no plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval benchmark for this model at all -- confirmed via the complete href list of every benchmark tile on the model's Vals AI page (only mmlu_pro, gpqa, lcb, mmmu, swebench, terminal-bench-2-1, and a set of domain-specific finance/legal/medical evals are present; no mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval tile exists). All four (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) are therefore left omitted_no_evidence. Added livecodebench 88.48% from Vals AI (rank 2/127) since its 'lcb' tile is confirmed via direct page-title fetch to be plain 'LiveCodeBench' (percentage pass rate), not the Pro/Elo variant already tracked. For mmmu: Vals AI's 'MMMU' tile (href /benchmarks/mmmu) was directly fetched and its own page <title> reads 'MMMU Pro' -- i.e. it actually measures the same MMMU-Pro protocol already captured by this model's existing mmmu-pro entry (80.5, self-reported), not base MMMU. No genuine base-MMMU score was found for this model from any source checked, so mmmu is left omitted_no_evidence rather than double-counting a Pro-protocol result under the wrong slug. (This same vals.ai MMMU/MMMU-Pro mislabeling was found to affect several other files elsewhere in the corpus -- flagged separately for a dedicated cleanup pass, not touched here.)
